data.body:
G4 (child consistency) container review — parent epic `5fd2` × child `75a9` (Pass-4 affirmative coach). Audited all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster; high-confidence + benign-reading filter applied. ONE finding (conflicting assumption: child Scope vs parent), then the affirmed clean modes.

═══ FINDING — Mode 2 CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS (child Scope contradicts the parent's Pass-4 input contract: "advisory-only" vs "blocking may also be coached") ═══
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child `75a9` §Scope ("INPUT is the CAPPED surfaced advisory set … BLOCKING findings are surfaced UNCAPPED and **may also be coached**.") vs parent `5fd2` Design-summary §Pass-4 ("a fourth pass AFTER Pass-3, over only the SURVIVING (advisory, not-dropped) findings") + parent §Review-structure Pass-4 ("a single-turn structured-output call over the SURVIVING (advisory) findings") + parent Pass-4 AC ("a single-turn structured-output call over the surviving findings"); also internally vs the child's own AC1/AC5 ("over the surviving (advisory) findings").
- evidence:
  - PARENT (both statements) constrains the Pass-4 INPUT to advisory/surviving findings ONLY: "over ONLY the SURVIVING (advisory, not-dropped) findings" / "over the SURVIVING (advisory) findings."
  - CHILD Scope ADDS blocking findings to that input set: "BLOCKING findings are surfaced UNCAPPED and may also be coached." This is a scope EXPANSION of the coached set beyond what the parent specifies — the two are not reconcilable by benign reading (the parent's "only … advisory" and the child's "blocking … may also be coached" are mutually exclusive on the question "is a blocking finding ever fed to the coach?").
  - The child is INTERNALLY inconsistent too: AC1 ("over the surviving (advisory) findings"), AC5 ("Advisory only"), and §What ("over ONLY the surviving (advisory, not-dropped) findings") all match the PARENT (advisory-only); only the one Scope sentence diverges — so the Scope sentence is the outlier on both axes.
  - The implementation resolved it the PARENT's way, confirming the Scope sentence is the stale/incorrect one: per this ticket's own PASS-1 G6 review comment and the orchestrator wiring, `pass4_coach` is handed ONLY `surfaced` (advisory), where `surfaced = advisory[:advisory_cap]`; blocking findings live in a separate list never passed to the coach (orchestrator.py ~548-558). So the shipped contract is advisory-only (parent-consistent); the Scope prose was never made to agree.
- scenarios:
  - An implementer (or a future reviser) reading the child's Scope wires blocking findings into the coach input to satisfy "may also be coached"; a reviewer reading the parent + AC1/AC5 expects advisory-only. The divergence is a latent scope-overlap/contract ambiguity about which finding set the coach consumes — exactly the cross-artifact inconsistency G4 guards against between a parent and its child.
  - Because the cap is advisory-only and blocking is cap-exempt (487d/55de/ca03), "BLOCKING surfaced UNCAPPED" is true as a Pass-3 surfacing fact — but appending "and may also be coached" conflates the SURFACING contract (Pass-3, blocking exempt) with the COACHING INPUT contract (Pass-4, advisory-only per the parent), making the child read as if Pass-4 ranges over blocking too.
- impact: A parent↔child consistency defect (not a coverage gap): the child's Scope states a Pass-4 input contract that contradicts the parent's explicit "advisory/surviving-only" Pass-4 definition (and the child's own AC1/AC5). Low blast-radius (the shipped code follows the parent), but the spec text remains contradictory and could mislead a re-review or a project extending the coach.
- checklist_item:
  - [ ] Reconcile the child's Scope sentence with the parent's advisory-only Pass-4 contract and the child's own AC1/AC5: strike "and may also be coached" from "BLOCKING findings are surfaced UNCAPPED and may also be coached" (keep the true clause that blocking is surfaced uncapped/cap-exempt, which is a Pass-3 fact owned by 487d/55de), so the child unambiguously states the coach ranges over the SURVIVING (advisory) findings only — matching parent `5fd2` §Pass-4 and the shipped `pass4_coach` wiring.
- suggested_fix: Replace the Scope clause with: "INPUT is the CAPPED surfaced advisory set (Pass-3 surfaces the top-N advisory by priority score, default 10). BLOCKING findings are surfaced UNCAPPED by Pass-3 (cap-exempt) but are NOT coached — the coach ranges over the surviving (advisory, not-dropped) findings only (parent §Pass-4; AC1/AC5)." This removes the parent-contradiction without touching the (correct) blocking-is-cap-exempt invariant.

═══ AFFIRMED CLEAN MODES (the other 6) ═══
1. IMPLICIT SHARED STATE — none unowned/double-written. The one shared datum the coach touches, the per-finding `id`, has a SINGLE owner declared identically in parent + child + siblings: minted by orchestrator `ca03` (`mint_finding_id`, SOLE owner), CONSUMED by 75a9 (`finding_refs`) and `db7b` (sidecar records it). Parent ("MINTED by the orchestrator (ca03) … its SOLE owner; the Pass-4 coach (75a9) and the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (db7b) reference findings BY this id"), ca03 ("the SOLE owner of the id; the sidecar (db7b) and the coach (75a9) CONSUME it"), and db7b all agree — no double-write, non-circular.

3. DEPENDENCY GAP — none. The Pass-3→Pass-4 edge is present and correctly ordered: 75a9 carries `depends_on 487d` (Pass-3), and `relates_to ca03`/`db7b`/`9da1`. ca03's "What" + 487d's "What" both name the Pass-4 dispatch over the surviving set. No missing sequencing edge.

4. SCOPE OVERLAP — none. 75a9's slice (single-turn coach: map finding→move, validate subject, deterministic render, move registry) does not overlap any sibling: cap/priority = 487d, cap CONFIG value = 55de (out-of-epic owner, named), id-minting + Pass-4 DISPATCH = ca03, sidecar recording = db7b, Pass-1/2 findings/verification = 1913/acc1. The coach IMPLEMENTS the move layer; ca03 only DISPATCHES it — complementary, no double-ownership.

5. ORDERING VIOLATION — none. Per the parent's risk-ordered MVP build order, Pass-4 coach is explicitly the FAN-OUT layer added AFTER the proven thin slice (DET floor + ca03 + one single-turn criterion + Pass-1/2/3 + attestation + claim). Pass-4 runs after Pass-3 and never alters its decision (parent §Pass-4 "NEVER alters the Pass-3 decision"; child AC5; `depends_on 487d`). Correct order.

6. CONSUMER IMPACT — none beyond the Finding above. 75a9 is a terminal consumer: it consumes the surviving advisory set + the minted id and emits coaching into the same result/sidecar; it never feeds back into the block/advisory decision (advisory-only). db7b records the coaching `finding_refs` by the same id — consistent read-only consumption. The ONE consumer-contract divergence (which input set the coach consumes) is captured in the Finding.

7. RESIDUAL REFERENCES — none. Every sibling/artifact 75a9 references resolves to a live, named entity: `ca03` (id-minter, CLOSED), `487d` (Pass-3, CLOSED), `db7b` (sidecar, CLOSED), `55de` (cap config owner, linked epic), `9da1` (shared three-pass framework, linked). No removed/renamed artifact referenced. (Move id 10 being absent from the 1-9,11,12 enum, and the move-11 `has_children` trigger gating, are within-child structural/T8 concerns already logged on this ticket — not parent/sibling consistency contradictions, so they do not surface as G4 findings here.)

NET: ONE G4 finding — child `75a9` §Scope ("BLOCKING findings … may also be coached") contradicts the parent's explicit advisory/surviving-only Pass-4 input contract (and the child's own AC1/AC5). The shipped code follows the parent, so this is a spec-text reconciliation, not a behavioral defect. The other 6 interaction modes are clean and consistent with the parent and every interacting sibling (ca03/487d/db7b/55de/9da1).
